items.briefing:
Cybercriminals are ramping up attacks across the Asia-Pacific region, with Interpol reporting a sharp rise in cybercrime activity — a trend directly relevant to Australian organisations. Two new ransomware threats have also emerged: the "Gentlemen" gang is equipping attackers with tools designed to disable security software before striking, while a new ransomware called Prinz Eugen targets your most recently modified files first, meaning active work documents are at greatest risk.

Separately, scammers are exploiting the FIFA World Cup 2026, using fake ticketing and streaming sites to steal personal and payment details from fans. Be cautious of unsolicited emails or social media links related to the tournament.

**Practical tip:** If you receive a World Cup-related offer or link, go directly to the official FIFA website rather than clicking any link in an email or message.

featured.title: ACSC warns of reported credential exposure affecting Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways
featured.quote: ACSC warns of critical Fortinet firewall vulnerability affecting Australian infrastructure security.
